name: LitServe (Lightning AI Model Serving)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Lightning AI (cloud hosting optional)

auth.notes: Optional API key auth via LitAPI.authorize(); default no auth

performance.notes: Built on FastAPI + Uvicorn; auto-batching; multi-worker support; 2x faster than FastAPI for ML inference

agent_readiness.notes: FastAPI-based ML serving with auto-batching and GPU acceleration. class SimpleLitAPI(ls.LitAPI): def setup(self, device): self.model = load_model(). def decode_request(self, request): return request['input']. def predict(self, x): return self.model(x). def encode_response(self, output): return {'output': output}. server = ls.LitServer(SimpleLitAPI(), accelerator='gpu', max_batch_size=32, batch_timeout=0.05). server.run(port=8000). Auto-batching aggregates requests for GPU efficiency. Streaming via yield. LLM server built-in. Compared to FastAPI: simpler batching, GPU lifecycle management, built-in health/metrics.

gotchas: Relatively new (2024) — smaller ecosystem than BentoML/TorchServe
Auto-batching adds latency for low-throughput scenarios
Less production-hardened than NVIDIA Triton for HPC
Lightning AI cloud integration is upsell — self-host requires ops knowledge
best_when: FastAPI-familiar teams needing GPU auto-batching without complexity
PyTorch model serving with minimal boilerplate
When BentoML/TorchServe are too complex for the use case
avoid_when: Multi-framework model serving (ONNX, TensorFlow, TensorRT) — use Triton
High-volume production at HPC scale (use Triton or TorchServe)
summary: LitServe is Lightning AI's FastAPI-based ML serving framework with auto-batching. Define setup(), decode_request(), predict(), encode_response() methods in a LitAPI subclass. LitServer(api, accelerator='gpu', max_batch_size=32) handles batching and GPU management. Built-in streaming (yield for SSE), health checks, OpenAPI spec. 2x faster than vanilla FastAPI for ML inference via auto-batching. Simpler than BentoML/TorchServe but less feature-complete.
